Formula: Total Debt = LongTermDebtNoncurrent + DebtCurrent (instant_sum). Non-current portion is long-term bonds/notes. Current portion (DebtCurrent) includes current maturities of long-term debt plus commercial paper when outstanding. CommercialPaper was $0 at FY2024 and FY2025 year-ends but may be non-zero during interim quarters (e.g., Q2 FY2025: $2,000M CP included in DebtCurrent $6,731M). LongTermDebt tag only has FY-level data (no quarterly) so cannot be used as a single tag.

item: Cash and Cash Equivalents Q4 uses FY-end balance
detail: FY2023=$7,079M, FY2024=$8,249M, FY2025=$14,712M; all from 10-K fiscal_year_end_balance

Intel uses CashCashEquivalentsRestrictedCashAndRestrictedCashEquivalents instead of CashAndCashEquivalentsAtCarryingValue used by QCOM/AMD/NVDA. For FY2025 year-end: broader tag = $14,712M vs narrow tag = $14,265M — difference of $447M (restricted cash, ~3%). The narrow tag has no data for FY2023 or FY2024 (Intel stopped reporting it ~FY2019, resumed Q2 FY2025). Broader tag chosen for coverage (10 of 12 quarters vs 3 of 12).

item: Cash and Cash Equivalents FY2025 Q2-Q3
detail: Broader cash tag has no Q2/Q3 entries for FY2025 (Intel switched back to narrow tag mid-year). Q1 available from broad tag; Q4 from FY-end balance. 2 of 12 quarters missing.
EPS cannot be derived by subtraction; 3 quarters flagged missing_requires_review. Would require earnings press releases as an independent source.

ASC 606 consolidated revenue tag. Same as AMD. Captures total Intel revenue across all segments (Client Computing, Data Center, Network & Edge, Mobileye, Foundry Services, etc.). Not a segment-only tag. SalesRevenueNet was used historically (FY2013-2017) but is discontinued; current tag has full coverage FY2021-2025.

item: Revenue tag change: SalesRevenueNet → ASC 606 tag
detail: Intel transitioned from SalesRevenueNet to RevenueFromContractWithCustomerExcludingAssessedTax around FY2018 (ASC 606 adoption). Our pipeline targets FY2023-2025 only, so this does not affect extraction. However, historical comparisons beyond FY2020 would require mapping the prior tag.

All CapEx values are positive (cash outflow reported as positive number). Same convention as AMD. No sign reversal needed. Values: FY2023 $25,750M, FY2024 $23,944M, FY2025 $14,646M. Intel's CapEx is 10-20× larger than fabless peers (QCOM/AMD/NVDA) due to semiconductor manufacturing (fabs).

Intel operates its own fabs, unlike QCOM/AMD/NVDA which are fabless or fab-lite. CapEx/Revenue ratios: FY2023=47%, FY2024=45%, FY2025=28%. This makes FCF structurally negative for most quarters. CapEx declined significantly in FY2025 ($14.6B vs $23.9B FY2024) as Intel scaled back some fab expansion plans. Direct peer CapEx comparison is misleading without adjusting for business model.

item: Intel uses 52/53-week fiscal year ending last Saturday of December
detail: FY2023 ended 2023-12-30 (363 days), FY2024 ended 2024-12-28 (363 days), FY2025 ended 2025-12-27 (363 days). FY2022 was 370 days (53-week year). Same fiscal calendar structure as AMD (both ~Dec 28).

item: Intel does not file a standalone Q4 10-Q
detail: Same pattern as all other pipeline companies: three 10-Qs (Q1-Q3) plus one 10-K.

item: Operating Income includes large restructuring/impairment charges
detail: FY2024 Q3 Operating Income = -$9,057M — extremely negative, likely driven by restructuring charges and asset impairments Intel announced in Aug-Oct 2024 (workforce reduction, foundry restructuring). FY2024 full-year Operating Income = -$11,678M. FY2023 Q1 also shows -$1,468M. These one-time charges make period-over-period operating income comparison unreliable without adjusting for non-recurring items. The pipeline correctly extracts the GAAP figures but does not normalize for restructuring.
informational
column7: Massive Q3 FY2024 net loss driven by asset impairments ($15.9B goodwill and intangible impairment announced Oct 2024) plus restructuring. FY2024 full-year net loss = -$18,756M. Arithmetic is correct but direct comparison to peers or prior periods is misleading without non-GAAP adjustments.

detail: Intel's quarterly gross margin ranges from 15% (Q3 FY2024) to 46% (Q4 FY2023). The Q3 FY2024 trough reflects elevated cost of revenue from charges. Fabless peers (QCOM ~56%, AMD ~49%, NVDA ~73%) have structurally higher gross margins because they do not carry manufacturing overhead.
